title: pittosporum fasciculatum
intro: Pittosporum fasciculatum, also known as the California black sage or California laurel, is a flowering evergreen plant native to the coastal scrub and chaparral habitats of California and Baja California. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of pittosporum fasciculatum include relief from bronchial spasms, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
